insight: During the match between Mexico and South Africa, the hydration breaks had a noticeable impact on the teams' attacking momentum. At the first break, neither team gained or lost any attacking momentum, as both Mexico and South Africa had the same number of actions before and after the break, resulting in no change in their attacking pace. However, at the second break, both teams experienced a slight decline in their attacking momentum, with Mexico's pace decreasing by 0.10 actions per minute and South Africa's pace also decreasing by 0.10 actions per minute, indicating that the break had a similar negative effect on both teams' attacking rhythm. Overall, neither team benefited more from the hydration breaks in terms of regaining or maintaining their attacking momentum, as both experienced a similar decline after the second break.
